subject: Project Management
title: Root Cause Analysis: The Five Whys
description: A quick win: understand Root Cause Analysis: The Five Whys.

hook.text: When a problem occurs at Toyota, they ask 'why?' five times. The real cause hides beneath the obvious one.

buildup.text: Why did the machine stop? Overloaded. Why? Not lubricated. Why? Pump failed. Why? Worn shaft. Why? No filter.

discovery.text: The fifth 'why' reaches the root cause: no filter on the pump. Fixing only the first symptom would fail.

twist.text: Most organizations fix symptoms and move on. The same problems return weeks later.

climax.text: Five Whys is deceptively simple. Its power lies in disciplined persistence — digging past the comfortable answer.

punchline.text: The first answer is never the real one. Keep asking.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of asking 'why' five times?
options: To reach the root cause hidden beneath surface symptoms
To annoy the person you're asking
